items.title: Credit and fraud scoring
items.description: Decision trees on transaction and customer features produce auditable approve/flag rules — regulators and analysts can trace each prediction through explicit split paths.

items.title: Medical triage and diagnosis support
items.description: Clinical decision support systems encode symptom and lab thresholds as tree rules; leaf probabilities guide referral even when a single 0.5 cutoff is not clinically optimal.

items.title: Customer segmentation and churn
items.description: Trees segment users by tenure, plan, and usage patterns; marketing teams read leaf profiles (e.g., "tenure < 3 months AND support_tickets > 5") for targeted retention.

items.title: Baseline before ensembles
items.description: A tuned single tree establishes an interpretable benchmark on tabular data before deploying random forests or gradient boosting for higher accuracy.

items.title: Feature importance screening
items.description: Split frequency and impurity decrease rank features used near the root and often — useful EDA before deeper modeling, though correlated features share importance.
